channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: YouTuber has DIMM idea, builds working DRAM in backyard
item.description: A YouTuber has successfully built working DRAM in his backyard as a response to rising memory prices driven by AI demand. This DIY project highlights the ongoing memory crisis and the lengths individuals may go to mitigate it.

item.title: Disable commit comments across your organization
item.description: GitHub has introduced a feature allowing organizations to disable commit comments across all repositories at once, streamlining management. This update simplifies the process for teams looking to maintain consistency in their coding practices.

item.title: Google explains why its all-in-one AI stack embraces competitors
item.description: Google Cloud's Andi Gutmans emphasized the company's unique position in the AI market, combining cloud infrastructure, AI models, and data platforms. This strategy aims to leverage AI for enterprise value while remaining open to competitors.

item.title: Amazon Quick for marketing: From scattered data to strategic action
item.description: Amazon Quick is a new tool designed to streamline marketing efforts by connecting various applications and learning user preferences. It aims to enhance productivity by creating a personalized knowledge graph.

item.title: Want free e-books? Stuff Your Kindle Day has 150+ titles discounted - today only
item.description: Today only, a sale offers over 150 free e-books for download on any e-reader, allowing users to keep them permanently. This event caters to avid readers looking for new content.

item.title: Why I recommend this $450 Samsung phone over competing models by Google and OnePlus
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy A37 is recommended for its superior camera features, despite facing tough competition from models by Google and OnePlus in the midrange smartphone market. Its value proposition makes it a noteworthy option.

item.title: Apple just fixed an iOS flaw exploited by the FBI - here's what happened
item.description: Apple's latest iOS update fixed a security flaw that allowed the FBI to access deleted messages from the Signal app. This fix underscores the importance of user privacy and security in mobile communications.

item.title: Age checks could turn internet into an ID checkpoint, complains Proton CEO
item.description: Proton's CEO has criticized proposed age verification measures, arguing they could transform the internet into an identity checkpoint rather than a safe space for minors. This raises concerns about privacy and accessibility online.

item.title: Applying multimodal biological foundation models across therapeutics and patient care
item.description: The article discusses the application of multimodal biological foundation models (BioFMs) in drug discovery and patient care, showcasing how AWS supports their development. This technology could revolutionize healthcare practices.

item.title: Better debugging with GitHub Copilot on the web
item.description: GitHub Copilot now offers improved debugging capabilities on the web, allowing users to quickly identify errors by recognizing stack traces more effectively. This enhancement aims to streamline the coding process.

item.title: How to easily encrypt your files on an Android phone - for free
item.description: A new app allows Android users to easily encrypt files for secure sharing, enhancing data protection on mobile devices. This is significant as data security becomes increasingly important.

item.title: My favorite MagSafe car charger easily handles bumpy roads (and it's on sale)
item.description: The Scosche MagicMount Charge Pro car charger is praised for its stability and fast charging capabilities, especially on bumpy roads. It's currently on sale, making it an attractive option for drivers.

item.title: Era raises $11M to build a software platform for AI gadgets
item.description: Era has raised $11 million to develop a software platform for various AI hardware gadgets, such as smart glasses and rings. This funding reflects the increasing interest in diverse AI applications.

item.title: Microsoft gives your Word documents an AI co-author you didn’t ask for
item.description: Microsoft is enhancing Word, Excel, and PowerPoint with a new AI feature called Copilot, which can now make edits autonomously. This marks a significant evolution in user assistance technology.

item.title: Datadog digs down into GPU efficiency as AI costs soar
item.description: Datadog has integrated GPU monitoring into its observability stack to help organizations manage rising AI costs. This tool provides insights into GPU efficiency during a time of increased demand.

item.title: 5 easy ways you wouldn't think to automate your home - but you really should
item.description: The article outlines five unconventional ways to automate home gadgets, resulting in a smoother and more efficient home environment. Home automation continues to gain traction among consumers.

item.title: The best SEO reporting software of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: A review of the best SEO reporting software for 2026 highlights tools like Semrush and Ahrefs, providing insights into effective SEO performance tracking. This is crucial for businesses aiming to improve their online presence.

item.title: Everpure 'takes the hit' as AI-fueled supply crunch drives prices up 70%
item.description: Everpure, formerly Pure Storage, is facing a 70% price increase due to an AI-driven supply crunch, which it predicts will persist beyond COVID disruptions. This situation raises concerns about storage costs in the tech industry.

item.title: OpenAI says its new GPT-5.5 model is more efficient and better at coding
item.description: OpenAI has announced its new GPT-5.5 model, claiming improved efficiency and coding capabilities over its predecessor. This development represents a significant advancement in AI language models.

item.title: Grab a ticket today: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 kicks off in just a week in San Francisco
item.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco is set to take place in a week, bringing together venture capitalists and founders for networking opportunities. This event is significant for the startup ecosystem.

item.title: Another customer of troubled startup Delve suffered a big security incident
item.description: Delve, a compliance company, was involved in a security incident with Context AI, highlighting vulnerabilities in AI startups. This incident raises concerns about data security in the tech industry.

item.title: Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope trumps Trump cuts, is launch-ready ahead of schedule
item.description: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope is ready for launch ahead of schedule, overcoming funding cuts from previous administrations. This telescope is expected to revolutionize our understanding of the universe.

item.title: Tag, You’re It: GeForce NOW Levels Up Game Discovery With Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ Labels
item.description: GeForce NOW has enhanced game discovery by integrating Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ labels, making it easier for gamers to access their favorite titles in the cloud. This upgrade aims to improve user experience and engagement.

item.title: Making Sense of the Early Universe
item.description: AI and GPUs are playing a crucial role in analyzing vast amounts of cosmic data, aiding astronomers in understanding the early universe. This integration of technology is transforming astronomical research.

item.title: Here’s how our TPUs power increasingly demanding AI workloads.
item.description: Google's new video showcases how its TPUs are designed to handle increasingly demanding AI workloads, emphasizing the importance of specialized hardware in AI advancements.

item.title: Introducing GPT-5.5
item.description: OpenAI has introduced GPT-5.5, its most advanced model yet, which is faster and more capable of handling complex tasks such as coding and data analysis. This model represents a significant leap in AI capabilities.

item.title: You’re about to feel the AI money squeeze
item.description: OpenClaw users faced restrictions as Anthropic imposed limits on the viral AI agent tool due to pressure to reduce system strain. This reflects the growing challenges in managing AI resources.

item.title: Pausing new self-serve signups for GitHub Copilot Business
item.description: GitHub has paused new self-serve signups for Copilot Business to ensure a sustainable experience for users. This move indicates a focus on reliability amidst growing demand.

item.title: GPT-5.5 Bio Bug Bounty
item.description: The GPT-5.5 Bio Bug Bounty invites participants to identify safety risks in the new model, offering rewards for successful discoveries. This initiative aims to enhance the model's security.

item.title: ParaRNN: Large-Scale Nonlinear RNNs, Trainable in Parallel
item.description: Apple researchers have developed a method to train large-scale nonlinear RNNs in parallel, making them more efficient and scalable. This advancement opens new possibilities for AI architecture design.

item.title: OpenAI: Workspace Agents for Business
item.description: OpenAI has introduced workspace agents in ChatGPT that automate workflows across various enterprise tools like Slack and Google Drive, enhancing productivity with governance controls. This development signifies a shift towards more integrated AI solutions for business environments.

item.title: What did Starbucks change with ChatGPT drink customization? #food
item.description: Starbucks is testing a new feature in its app that uses ChatGPT to help customers customize their drink orders based on various factors. This innovation aims to enhance customer experience and engagement through personalized service.

item.title: The 10 NFL Draft Classes With The Highest Number Of Hall Of Famers
item.description: This article discusses NFL draft classes with the highest number of Hall of Famers, highlighting the talent evaluation process in football. It serves as a reference for fans and analysts interested in the history of the NFL.

item.title: Anthropic Mythos access controversy: what happened #tech
item.description: Anthropic is facing security issues as unauthorized users accessed its Claude Mythos model, which is designed for cybersecurity. This incident raises concerns about data protection and the integrity of AI systems.

item.title: Google's Gemini for Home Is Bringing Back Continued Conversations
item.description: Google's Gemini for Home is reintroducing the 'continued conversations' feature, allowing users to interact with their smart speakers without repeatedly saying 'Hey, Google.' This enhancement aims to improve user experience and engagement.

item.title: familybugle-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: The Family Bugle model has been added to PyPI, allowing integration with various AI clients. This development supports families in managing their communication needs through AI technology.

item.title: OpenAI updates ChatGPT with Codex-powered ‘workspace agents’ for teams
item.description: OpenAI has updated ChatGPT with new Codex-powered workspace agents for teams, allowing users to create custom tools within the app. This update represents a significant evolution in how teams can leverage AI for collaboration.

item.title: Using AI To Personalize Healthcare–Without Losing Patient Trust
item.description: This edition of InnovationRx explores the potential and challenges of using AI to personalize healthcare, improve clinical trials, and advance genetic medicine. It emphasizes the need for maintaining patient trust while leveraging AI technologies.

item.title: Milestone for machines: Robot beating human pros at table tennis...
item.description: A new study reveals that a robot designed for table tennis is successfully competing against and sometimes defeating elite human players, showcasing advancements in AI capabilities in sports. This milestone illustrates the growing prowess of AI in complex tasks.

item.title: Top MAGA Influencer's True Identity Exposed After Racking Up Millions of Followers with Patriotic Bikini Photos and Gun-Toting Content
item.description: An influencer, who gained popularity with patriotic content, has been revealed to be an AI creation designed to mislead followers. This incident raises questions about authenticity and the impact of AI on social media.

item.title: How to transform document activation workflows with Genie and Agent Bricks
item.description: Enterprises can enhance their document workflows by integrating AI/BI Genie, Agent Bricks, and Unity Catalog, reducing compliance risks and improving efficiency.

item.title: Typeface Brings Agentic Marketing Orchestration to Google Cloud
item.description: Typeface has launched Agentic Marketing Orchestration on Google Cloud, enhancing marketing capabilities for businesses using AI-driven solutions.

item.title: Amazon SageMaker AI now supports serverless model customization for Qwen3.5 models
item.description: Amazon SageMaker AI now allows serverless customization for Qwen3.5 models, enabling users to fine-tune models with advanced techniques.

item.title: Coding Models Are Doing Too Much
item.description: The article discusses the overreach of coding models, suggesting they may be taking on too much responsibility in software development.

item.title: BMW unveils the new i7 with nearly 450 miles of range and Neue Klasse style [Images]
item.description: BMW has revealed its revamped i7 electric sedan for 2027, featuring nearly 450 miles of range and new technology.

item.title: MAGA's gun-toting bikini queen exposed as an AI fraud milking lonely conservative men
item.description: A popular figure in the MAGA movement has been exposed as an AI-generated persona, raising questions about authenticity in online identities.

item.title: Tredence Brings Enterprise AI to Action with Google Cloud's Gemini-Powered Agentic Accelerators | Biz News Desk
item.description: Tredence and Google Cloud are launching Agentic Accelerators to enhance enterprise AI capabilities, focusing on data modernization and decision-making.

item.title: Pichai opens Cloud Next 2026 with $240B backlog, 750M Gemini users, and a plan to turn Search into an agent manager
item.description: Sundar Pichai announced major growth for Google Cloud at Cloud Next 2026, including a $240 billion backlog and plans to enhance Search with AI.

item.title: Don't Wait: Samsung Messages Is About to Disappear on Your Galaxy Phone
item.description: Samsung is discontinuing its Messages app, urging users to switch to Google Messages before the deadline in July.

item.title: ooai-llm 0.3.0
item.description: The ooai-llm 0.3.0 update introduces new features like typed LLM settings and metadata enrichment for enhanced functionality.
